mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.09% signals an extended zero-rate monetary regime. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates. Sustained low FEDFUNDS (below 0.5%) produces positive S&P 500 forward returns as the cost of capital for equities remains suppressed relative to historical norms.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 7.6% signals labor market slack. When UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, firms face reduced wage pressure and improved profit margins. This margin expansion drives positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
regime_hint: labor market slack with elevated unemployment
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%) when UNRATE > 7.0%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 422,794 million dollars combined with housing starts (HOUST) at 914 thousand units indicate consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, aggregate demand expansion drives positive equity returns within 20 trading days through improved earnings visibility.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST grow month-over-month, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 98.67 (below 2017=100 baseline) signals supply-side weakness. When INDPRO declines below 99.0, constrained supply relative to demand raises inflation expectations and compresses real equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO < 99.0, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI (CPIAUCSL) at 231.831 combined with FEDFUNDS at 0.09% creates a large negative real rate environment. When nominal rates remain below inflation, real purchasing power erodes and equity risk premiums compress. This regime produces positive equity returns as investors seek real asset protection over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: negative real rate regime with monetary accommodation
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly positive (mean < 1.0%) when real rates are negative, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 135,902 thousand persons combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE at 7.6%) indicates labor market tightening. When PAYEMS grows month-over-month while UNRATE falls, wage inflation accelerates and equity valuations compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month and UNRATE falls
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with wage pressure
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS grows and UNRATE falls, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 13,725.7 billion chained 2017 dollars represents the broadest demand signal. When GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized (quarter-over-quarter), economic momentum weakens and equity earnings expectations decline, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: sub-trend economic growth with deceleration risk
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) reveals supply-side capacity utilization. When INDPRO growth decelerates while PAYEMS remains flat or declining, productive capacity is underutilized and profit margins compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.1% and PAYEMS flat or declining
regime_hint: supply-side slack with underutilized labor and capacity
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS show weakness, or interaction Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.
